2603.23862.title: Deep Convolutional Neural Networks for predicting highest priority functional group in organic molecules
2603.23862.paper_summary: Our work addresses the problem of predicting the highest priority functional group present in an organic molecule. Functional Groups are groups of bound atoms that determine the physical and chemical properties of organic molecules. In the presence of multiple functional groups, the dominant functional group determines the compound's properties. Fourier-transform Infrared spectroscopy (FTIR) is a commonly used spectroscopic method for identifying the presence or absence of functional groups within a compound. We propose the use of a Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to predict the highest priority functional group from the Fourier-transform infrared spectrum (FTIR) of the organic molecule. We have compared our model with other previously applied Machine Learning (ML) method Support Vector Machine (SVM) and reasoned why CNN outperforms it.
2603.23862.paper_summary_zh: 我們的工作針對預測有機分子中存在的最高優先級官能團的問題。<lb/>官能團是決定有機分子物理和化學性質的束縛原子組。<lb/>在存在多個官能團的情況下，主導官能團決定化合物的性質。<lb/>傅立葉變換紅外光譜（FTIR）是一種常用的光譜學方法，用於識別化合物中官能團的存在或缺失。<lb/>我們提出使用深度卷積神經網絡（CNN）來預測有機分子的傅立葉變換紅外光譜（FTIR）中的最高優先級官能團。<lb/>我們將我們的模型與其他先前應用的機器學習（ML）方法支持向量機（SVM）進行了比較，並推理為什麼CNN的表現優於它。
2603.23862.author: Kunal Khatri et.al.
